title: Folder visibility when there's no items in the folder
excerpt:
Issue 
When there’s no items in the folder, users (members and workspace owners) cannot see the folder. Only the folder creator can see the folder when the folder is empty. I know this is a feature, not a bug, but it doe…

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Issue
When there’s no items in the folder, users (members and workspace owners) cannot see the folder. Only the folder creator can see the folder when the folder is empty. I know this is a feature, not a bug, but it doesn’t work.

Request
We need to see folders in order to move items into them. For example, this is our folder structure:

  • Unassigned
  • Assigned
  • Complete

People cannot move items into the folders if they cannot see the folder.

posts.author: rhavens_EPI (Randy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed! I’m trying to create a Change Management workspace with a similar folder structure:

  • Needs Approval
  • In Progress
  • Final Review
  • Completed
    • 2025
  • Denied
  • Reverted

My whole plan centered around moving changes in and out of these folders as they progress though the process. People can’t move items into the appropriate folder if they can’t see the folders! I need an option to allow people to see these folders.

posts.author: DavidDvorak (David)
posts.cookedHtml: +1, it’s a little silly that people can’t see a folder because it has no contents. not the way it works on any other software that uses a folder concept. would hope this is an easy update that can be prioritized soon. Causes a lot of confusion among my people

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml: Another pointer is, how can people use a folder if they can’t see it? The folder would remain empty.
